summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a low-privileged global member can list log-streaming destinations and recover secret-bearing webhook configuration data from the API response.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should allow a global member account to read a webhook destination whose serialized response still contains the secret Authorization header Bearer SECRET123.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh from the final session-root finding folder cloned n8n, checked out the vulnerable 2.14.0 code at commit 2374f40ec36795904b4011fb81018da818a76ee8, built image tag evohunt-advisory_4c45c5a8-log_streaming_destination_secret_exposure, executed the targeted integration test, printed EVOHUNT_LOG_STREAMING_SECRET_LEAK_CONFIRMED, printed {"destinationId":"1784d732-2c70-4bfe-8436-70927e0909ec","leakedJsonHeaders":"{\"Authorization\":\"Bearer SECRET123\"}"}, and finished with PASS test/integration/evohunt-log-streaming-secret-leak.test.ts plus the reproduction success banner.
verification_method: The helper test creates a webhook log-streaming destination as the owner with a secret Authorization header, lists destinations as a global member, locates the created destination, asserts that jsonHeaders still contains Bearer SECRET123, and emits a proof string only when the member-visible response includes the leaked secret.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
